content: Clinical management requires recognizing CYP inhibitors and inducers. Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors (increase substrate levels): clarithromycin, itraconazole, ritonavir, grapefruit juice - avoid with simvastatin, contraindicated with some antiarrhythmics. Moderate inhibitors: fluconazole, diltiazem, verapamil. Strong inducers (decrease substrate levels): rifampin, carbamazepine, phenytoin, St. John's wort - can reduce oral contraceptive efficacy, transplant rejection risk with cyclosporine. CYP2D6 poor metabolizers: codeine ineffective (needs conversion to morphine); tamoxifen reduced efficacy; atomoxetine toxicity. CYP2D6 ultrarapid metabolizers: codeine toxicity risk. CYP2C19 poor metabolizers: clopidogrel ineffective - use prasugrel/ticagrelor. CYP2C9 poor metabolizers: warfarin sensitivity - lower doses needed.
